name: BTC 4h Compression Fakeout Re-Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression resolves one way, that move fails, and the opposite direction is entered once directional control reasserts with a simpler confirmation sequence than prior dead squeeze logic
thesis: Cycle 76 includes compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation. BTC 4h is the best-fit lane because its 20-bar regime mix is mostly transition, which is exactly where fake release then true expansion can occur. The key lesson from cycle 75 is to simplify confirmation and avoid the dead squeeze-persistence grammar.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 4h fakeout-to-go sequences still produce 0 trades or if the simplified confirmation enters too late to monetize, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility compresses, one side breaks and fails, the opposite side takes control, and expansion follows.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Compression often traps premature breakout participation. When the first move fails in a transition regime, the second move can travel farther because trapped inventory helps fuel it.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because it is transition-heavy enough for real second-leg expansion while still liquid and stable enough to keep the structure interpretable.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The fakeout may not be distinct enough, causing either late entry or undertrading.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but runners truncate, extend the trail before widening the compression detector.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than ETH reacceptance, but stronger asymmetry when the second-leg expansion is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h clears density and the simplified fakeout confirmation produces materially more participation than the dead persistence-release family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or fakeout-confirmation entries that reverse immediately on most occurrences.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but improved because confirmation is simpler than prior squeeze-persistence logic

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for compression fakeout into re-expansion.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough fakeout-to-go sequences with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h still returns 0 trades or immediate reversals after confirmation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a minority of stronger expansion legs carrying the expectancy.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same simplified fakeout grammar gains density on 1h without degenerating into noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count while preserving fakeout-to-go structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h becomes noisy and loses the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller second-leg expansions than 4h.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether stronger trend contamination helps the same fakeout-to-go grammar.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows fewer but stronger fakeout reversals into expansion.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h turns the setup into late chasing again.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, larger expansions than BTC.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
